Downloads the selected architecture build of Zed from the project's GitHub releases.

rather than a universal binary. DOWNLOAD_TYPE selects which upstream asset to fetch
(aarch64 or x86_64; default aarch64); ARCHITECTURE is the normalised label used for
the package name downstream (arm64 or x86_64). The app version is read from the
mounted app rather than the release tag.

by ETag/Last-Modified and the StopProcessingIf download_changed predicate
short-circuits the chain on no-op re-runs.

members.description:
Downloads a linked item from an Apple Support article and verifies

Downloads the latest version of a particular Apple Support Article (specified in the input keys)
and, if it has updated, copies it to the designated directory as HTML, marked with the modification date
(ISO 8601 date format).

Input Keys:
    ARTICLE is the Apple Support Article number. This is often just six digits, although certain pages
        still use the two letters (HT) plus six digits format (w.g., Apple security releases).
    LANG is the language and country descriptor, consisting of two letters for the language, a dash, and
        two letters for the country (e.g., en-us). Many will want to force the US version in order to get
        the earliest indication of a change.
    SAVE_LOCATION is the path to the directory where the HTML file should be saved. The path should exist.

sets.skip_reason: These are fundamentally different recipes that happen to share a name. davidbpirie-recipes downloads a linked binary/pkg from an Apple Support article and verifies its code signature (has CodeSignatureVerifier, designed for downloading Apple software updates). jazzace-recipes downloads the HTML content of an Apple Support article, saves it as an HTML file with a date stamp, and uses Copier to archive it (no CodeSignatureVerifier, designed for monitoring article changes). They serve entirely different purposes: one downloads software, the other archives web content.

Downloads the current release version of Eclipse Temurin 11. This is the signed pkg version rather 
than the tar version, for those that require a signed package rather than accurate version number.

FEATURE_VERSION may be any valid whole number, e.g. 8, 11, 16, 17. See https://api.adoptium.net/v3/info/available_releases for available feature versions.

RELEASE_TYPE can be 'ga' (general availablility) or 'ea' (early access).

OS may be 'mac' or others.

ARCH may be 'x64' (or 'arm' for some builds).

IMAGE_TYPE may be 'jdk', 'jre', 'testimage', 'debugimage' or 'staticlibs'.

JVM_IMPLEMENTATION may be "hotspot", "openj9" or "dragonwell".

HEAP_SIZE may be 'normal' or 'large'.

VENDOR may be 'adoptopenjdk', 'openjdk', 'adoptium', 'alibaba', 'ibm'.

Note that not all options are available for all vendors, and not all listed vendors may be currently available.

Downloads the current release version of Eclipse Temurin 8. This is based from the tar version rather than the signed pkg version, so that the true version number can be obtained.

FEATURE_VERSION may be any valid whole number, e.g. 8, 11, 16, 17. See https://api.adoptium.net/v3/info/available_releases for available feature versions.

RELEASE_TYPE can be 'ga' (general availablility) or 'ea' (early access).

OS may be 'mac' or others.

DOWNLOAD_ARCH may be 'x64' or 'aarch64' for macOS-specific builds. Note that if you change this, you MUST also change the supported_architectures in the pkginfo dictionary to match what Munki expects: https://github.com/munki/munki/wiki/Pkginfo-Files#supported_architectures

IMAGE_TYPE may be 'jdk', 'jre', 'testimage', 'debugimage' or 'staticlibs'.

JVM_IMPLEMENTATION may be "hotspot", "openj9" or "dragonwell".

HEAP_SIZE may be 'normal' or 'large'.

VENDOR may be 'adoptopenjdk', 'openjdk', 'adoptium', 'alibaba', 'ibm'.

Note that not all options are available for all vendors, and not all listed vendors may be currently available.

Downloads the latest arm64 (Apple Silicon) release of Codex CLI from GitHub.

Uses GitHubReleasesInfoProvider for authenticated API calls (avoids the
60-req/hour unauthenticated rate limit on shared CI runners), then
FindAndReplace strips the "rust-v" prefix from the tag so %version% is

codesig_authorities: identifier "codex" and anchor apple generic and certificate 1[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.2.6] /* exists */ and certificate leaf[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.1.13] /* exists */ and certificate leaf[subject.OU] = "2DC432GLL2"
members.description:
Downloads the latest Codex CLI (Apple Silicon and Intel) from the openai/codex
GitHub releases and verifies each binary's code signature.

Codex publishes per-architecture tarballs (codex-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz and
codex-x86_64-apple-darwin.tar.gz). Both are downloaded and extracted so the pkg
recipe can build a universal installer. The clean version is derived in the pkg
recipe from the binary itself, because the GitHub release tag is prefixed
(e.g. rust-vX.Y.Z).

Run as part of the full download -> pkg -> upload chain. URLDownloaderPython caches
by ETag/Last-Modified and the StopProcessingIf download_changed predicate
short-circuits the chain on no-op re-runs.

codesig_authorities: identifier "com.postgresapp.Postgres2" and anchor apple generic and certificate 1[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.2.6] /* exists */ and certificate leaf[field.1.2.840.113635.100.6.1.13] /* exists */ and certificate leaf[subject.OU] = "ZF84SJ5A3G"
members.description:
Downloads the selected PostgreSQL major-version build of Postgres.app from the
project's GitHub releases.

Run as part of the full download -> pkg -> upload chain. URLDownloaderPython
caches by ETag/Last-Modified and the StopProcessingIf download_changed predicate
short-circuits the chain on no-op re-runs.

Postgres.app publishes one DMG per PostgreSQL major version (e.g. 14-18) plus a
combined all-versions DMG. POSTGRES_VERSION selects which single-version DMG to
fetch (default 18). asset_regex is anchored so it matches only the single-version
asset and never the combined "-14-15-16-17-18" build. The app version is read
from the mounted app rather than the release tag.
